lockedMeasurements: Release boundary: source commit 5a33dfb38276b9edbc9eaba769d8de6380c191ae and active staging Worker 7a16645f-d307-48f0-9ad5-8616cf9cd196.
The Stage 8 semantic ledger records three complete workflows, twelve OpenAI calls, no fallback, median cost $0.237971, and p95 cost $0.254133 using the specified model routing.
The deployed student snapshot is at Lock with fresh inheritance, three live bets, $0.74 usage, a selected staged operating-partner bet, and three saved versions.

The deployed instructor snapshot shows absent Module 1 inheritance, the staged operating-partner bet locked, but the deterministic ranking places the internal escalation cadence first and the selected bet second.
Confidence is contained in the reviewed state, UI, saved artifact, and instructor paths; only ordinary deterministic comparison values are released.
Generated options remain provisional until the dedicated server admission transition, and model evaluation does not directly set selection, loss bearer, accountability, or reversibility.
Instructor queries and artifact routes are class-scoped; prompt history is closed by default and requires two disclosure actions in the supplied UI.
The exact saved PDF is eight letter-sized pages and contains all 217 substantive document strings with no missing leaves or broken glyphs.

unknowns.attackQuestion: Can a student with no Module 1 inheritance reach Lock and save a recommendation?
unknowns.requiredWitness: Deployed student and instructor browser snapshots created 2026-07-14T07:28:04.809Z.

unknowns.attackQuestion: Does the exact saved PDF preserve the structured recommendation content?
unknowns.requiredWitness: Exact saved PDF text, eight rendered pages, and deterministic PDF regression measurement.

unknowns.attackQuestion: Does the client brief describe the selected bet consistently with the current deterministic ranking?
unknowns.requiredWitness: Deployed instructor snapshot plus exact saved PDF.

unknowns.attackQuestion: Does a one-bet field automatically enter the required factory-mode pick-list?
unknowns.requiredWitness: Frozen src/module2-page.js and src/module2-engine.js, including groundGo, boardBlockers, and rankLiveBets.

unknowns.attackQuestion: Does weakField physically block Lock as required by the wireframe?
unknowns.requiredWitness: Frozen src/module2-engine.js rankLiveBets and src/module2-page.js boardBlockers/renderBoardAction.

unknowns.attackQuestion: Does a slider weight of zero actually remove that criterion from the deterministic comparison?
unknowns.requiredWitness: Frozen src/module2-page.js slider range and src/module2-engine.js normalizedWeights.

unknowns.attackQuestion: Is the product assuming that a later option picker exists after the user chooses Pick later?
unknowns.requiredWitness: Frozen src/module2-page.js groundGo request and src/module2-state.js combineGroundSolutions.

unknowns.attackQuestion: Is the product assuming that an already confirmed frame remains valid after the reply is corrected?
unknowns.requiredWitness: Frozen src/module2-page.js module2FrameNeedsExplicitReview and reconcile-reply transition.

unknowns.attackQuestion: Can client-controlled draft state be recombined with server ranking and packaging to launder forged relevance, evidence, criteria, or coverage?
unknowns.requiredWitness: Frozen src/studio.js handleSaveModule2Workspace, handleRerankModule2, handleModule2Judgments, and module2 package readiness logic.

unknowns.attackQuestion: Can irrelevant or injection-shaped text obtain model access by wrapping itself in Bethany assignment language?
unknowns.requiredWitness: Frozen src/studio.js detectAssignmentAbuse/checkModelAccess and Module 2 payload construction.

unknowns.attackQuestion: Does confirming multiple attributed voices change the comparison before the recommendation can be locked?
unknowns.requiredWitness: Stage 8 forwarded-two-voice ledger and frozen src/module2-page.js voice-yes handler.

unknowns.attackQuestion: Can complete structured content still render as an unpolished client artifact through orphaned headings?
unknowns.requiredWitness: Rendered page 1 of the exact saved PDF and src/module2-pdf.js addLabelValue pagination logic.

probes.hypothesis: The candidate source lacks the required single-column mobile layout and sticky primary action.
probes.finding: Closes this failure hypothesis at source level: the responsive breakpoint, single-column grids, and sticky action are present.

probes.hypothesis: Correcting a forwarded-thread extraction avoids manipulating a large free-text block on a phone.
probes.witness: Frozen src/module2-page.js renderReplyRow and authoritative mobile extraction wireframe.
probes.finding: Confirms the failure hypothesis: the only correction control is a prefilled full-reply textarea; no tap-to-highlight interaction is implemented, leaving avoidable full-thread manipulation on mobile.

probes.hypothesis: Fresh entry without Module 1 inheritance is a terminal dead end.
probes.witness: Deployed student and instructor browser snapshots showing fresh/absent inheritance, Lock, and three saved versions.
probes.finding: Closes this broad hypothesis: a fresh student did reach Lock and save artifacts.

probes.hypothesis: A student entering with one bet receives the automatic provisional factory pick-list required by the specification.
probes.witness: Frozen src/module2-page.js groundGo and boardBlockers plus src/module2-engine.js rankLiveBets.
probes.finding: Confirms the failure hypothesis: one bet is not auto-expanded; the primary recovery offers repeated evaluation even though ranking requires two admitted bets. Suggest options remains a separate optional control.

probes.hypothesis: Selecting Pick later leads to an actual chooser without discarding the comparison field.
probes.witness: Frozen src/module2-page.js groundGo, which sends no pickedIds, and src/module2-state.js combineGroundSolutions, which filters pick mode by an empty pickedIds set.
probes.finding: Confirms the failure hypothesis: Pick later has no later picker and deterministically returns an empty set.

probes.hypothesis: Derived relevance, evaluation, provenance, ranking, and coverage cannot be forged through the ordinary workspace save API.
probes.finding: Confirms the failure hypothesis: ordinary PUT state accepts client-authored ground and ranking fields and student-origin evaluation records. The rank and package paths can then trust those records without rerunning provenance admission.

probes.hypothesis: Dominated live foils trigger the required weak-field hard stop.
probes.witness: Frozen src/module2-engine.js rankLiveBets and src/module2-page.js boardBlockers/renderBoardAction.
probes.finding: Confirms the failure hypothesis: weakField is computed but never rendered or enforced, so a field with fewer than two non-dominated alternatives can proceed to Lock.

probes.hypothesis: Reweighting cannot silently preserve a criterion set to zero or bypass a newly created near tie.
probes.witness: Frozen src/module2-engine.js normalizedWeights and src/module2-page.js renderBoardAction/rerank transition.
probes.finding: Confirms the failure hypothesis: zero is replaced by the equal-weight default through `supplied.get(...) || default`; a near-tie warning is also suppressed when an earlier selectedBetId remains set after reranking.

probes.hypothesis: Irrelevant or prompt-injection use is rejected before external model access even when assignment words are included.
probes.finding: Confirms the failure hypothesis: free-chat or unrelated patterns are rejected only when no assignment term appears. Adding Bethany or decision language defeats the pre-call rejection.

probes.hypothesis: Candidate confidence can be injected into state, UI, instructor output, or the saved PDF.
probes.witness: Frozen module2-containment and confidence regression suites, deployed UI snapshots, and exact saved PDF.
probes.finding: Closes the confidence-manipulation hypothesis: injected confidence fields are dropped or stripped and no confidence claim appears in the released artifact.

probes.hypothesis: Human confirmation of attributed voice disagreement causes reevaluation before Lock.
probes.witness: Stage 8 forwarded-two-voice ledger SHA-256 fa3dc22f4d63fe02e5722da7223d7ec995576b0444df4a1ca425c31efd963f68 and frozen src/module2-page.js voice-yes handler.
probes.finding: Confirms the failure hypothesis: Stage 8 stops at humanConfirmed=false, while the supplied transition only appends fog and saves; it does not reevaluate, rerank, or block Lock.

probes.hypothesis: Correcting the reply invalidates an earlier frame confirmation when the new reconciliation reports drift.
probes.witness: Frozen src/module2-page.js module2FrameNeedsExplicitReview and reconcile-reply flow, with src/module2-engine.js applyReconciliation.
probes.finding: Confirms the failure hypothesis: an existing confirmed/revised value suppresses explicit frame review even after upstream reply changes, and reconciliation does not clear that judgment.

probes.hypothesis: Instructor data, prompt records, versions, PDFs, and class ZIPs are unscoped or disclosed by default.
probes.witness: Frozen instructor-page.js, instructor-queries.js, studio.js authorization routes, regression tests, and deployed instructor snapshot.
probes.finding: Closes the cross-student/default-disclosure hypothesis for the supplied paths: class membership checks are present, prompt history is closed, and workflow-specific versions and ZIP actions are available.

probes.hypothesis: The selected student's recommendation is semantically consistent across ranking, Lock, instructor view, and PDF.
probes.witness: Deployed instructor snapshot and exact saved PDF text/pages.
probes.finding: Confirms the failure hypothesis: the ranking places the cadence first and the selected staged role second, while the PDF says the selected role currently leads and is the strongest recommendation. No conviction-evidence override is explained.

probes.hypothesis: The exact PDF is missing structured document leaves or has broken glyphs.
probes.witness: Eight rendered pages, exact extracted text, and the 217-string deterministic measurement.
probes.finding: Closes this artifact-loss hypothesis: all substantive leaves are present and legible.

probes.hypothesis: Complete PDF content is paginated with headings kept with their bodies.
probes.witness: Rendered page 1 of the exact saved PDF and frozen src/module2-pdf.js addLabelValue.
probes.finding: Confirms the failure hypothesis: “Why this recommendation currently leads” is orphaned at the bottom of page 1 while its body begins on page 2.

coverageArgument: The inquiry locked release identity, deployed student behavior, deployed instructor behavior, live-model semantics, deterministic transitions, authorization, and the exact released artifact before attacking unknowns.
Utility coverage includes fresh entry, one-bet factory mode, merge/pick semantics, mobile correction, Board recovery, Lock, and PDF generation.
Correctness coverage follows upstream reply changes through reconciliation, voice handling, weights, ranking, human selection, package compilation, and rendered client prose.

Security coverage includes irrelevant use, prompt-injection admission, confidence manipulation, client-state tampering, server-side judgment gates, class scoping, prompt disclosure, and PDF authorization.
Artifact coverage checks both leaf completeness and semantic/pagination quality against the exact saved eight-page PDF rather than compiler intent.
All five required perspectives are represented inside this single inquiry. Positive closures do not offset the independently confirmed release-blocking failures.

complaints: Forwarded-reply correction requires manipulating the full raw reply in a textarea rather than the specified highlight or purpose-built relevant-lines interaction.
A completed brief can claim that the selected recommendation leads even when the visible deterministic ranking places it second.
evidence: Frozen src/module2-page.js renderReplyRow and authoritative mobile wireframe.
Deployed instructor snapshot and exact saved PDF.

complaints: The required automatic one-bet factory mode is absent; following the primary Evaluate recovery cannot produce a valid ranking.
Pick later has no chooser and empties the comparison field.
evidence: Deployed fresh-inheritance student completed Lock and three versions.
Frozen src/module2-page.js groundGo/boardBlockers and src/module2-state.js combineGroundSolutions.

complaints: Client-controlled state can forge derived relevance, evidence, evaluation status, criteria, coverage, and ranking before explicit judgment and packaging calls.
Weak-field padding is computed but not blocked.
Zero-weight and stale near-tie behavior permit weight gaming.
Irrelevant or injection-shaped requests can obtain model access when wrapped in assignment language.
evidence: Frozen src/studio.js workspace-save, rank, judgment, readiness, and abuse-control paths.
Frozen src/module2-engine.js rankLiveBets/normalizedWeights and src/module2-page.js renderBoardAction.
Confidence containment tests separately close confidence injection.

complaints: Confirming multiple attributed voices does not reevaluate or rerank the bets before Lock.
Correcting the reply does not invalidate a prior frame confirmation, so newly detected drift can be carried through without renewed judgment.
evidence: Stage 8 forwarded-two-voice ledger records only an unconfirmed signal.
Frozen src/module2-page.js voice and reply-correction transitions plus applyReconciliation behavior.

complaints: The selected student's ranking and recommendation brief contradict each other without explaining a human override.
The exact saved PDF contains an orphaned section heading on page 1.
evidence: Deployed instructor panel shows the selected bet ranked second.
Exact saved PDF calls that bet the current leader and strongest recommendation.
Rendered PDF page 1 separates the heading from its body.

utilityFindings: Pick later is a broken interaction: the client sends no pickedIds and no later picker exists, so pick mode returns an empty comparison set.
The n=1 path does not invoke automatic factory mode; its primary Evaluate recovery cannot satisfy the two-admitted-bet ranking invariant.
The forwarded-reply repair uses a full raw-reply textarea and omits the specified tap-to-highlight interaction, creating avoidable mobile manipulation.
qualityFindings: The exact PDF orphans “Why this recommendation currently leads” at the bottom of page 1.
Client-facing prose says the selected bet currently leads while the same artifact numbers it second in the deterministic candidate field.
The Lock/package experience does not explain why human conviction overrides the evidence ranking.
correctnessFindings: The deployed ranking is cadence first and staged operating partner second, but the package describes the selected second-ranked bet as the current leader and strongest recommendation.
Voice confirmation changes fog state without triggering reevaluation or ranking refresh.
An upstream reply correction can leave frameConfirmation valid even when the new frame status is drift or thin.
A displayed weight of zero is converted back to a nonzero equal default.
weakField is calculated but is absent from Board blockers and package readiness.
A previously selected bet suppresses the near-tie choice after reranking.
Package readiness does not require the set-completeness judgment itself; forged covered status can therefore bypass that gate.
securityFindings: The ordinary workspace PUT treats substantial derived state as client-authoritative. Forged student-origin evaluations and provenance can reach deterministic ranking and client packaging without passing applyBetEvaluations validation.
The irrelevant-use guard is bypassed whenever free-chat or injection patterns coexist with Bethany or other assignment terms, allowing irrelevant external model access.
Server readiness does not independently enforce weak-field, renewed near-tie, voice-resolution, or set-confirmation invariants, so browser controls are not a sufficient security boundary.
artifactFindings: The PDF completeness witness passes: eight pages, 217 substantive strings, zero missing leaves, and no broken glyphs.
The released artifact is semantically inconsistent about which option leads.
The released artifact has an avoidable orphan heading on page 1.
residualRisks: The specification's accepted clean-case soft-frame limitation remains non-actionable here; the confirmed stale-confirmation defect is separate and requires correction.
Heuristic relevance and voice detection can still misfire as explicitly named in the specification; they remain acceptable only when their human recovery transitions are correctly enforced.
Cohort homogenization remains the specification's named pedagogy risk rather than a release blocker.
requiredChanges: Make reconciliation, evidence provenance, evaluation status, coverage, duplicate decisions, and ranking server-owned. Reject client mutation or revalidate them on every rank and package transition.
Enforce all judgment invariants server-side, including set-completeness confirmation, weak-field review, post-rerank near-tie choice, and the conviction-evidence check for a non-leading selection.

Invalidate dependent frame, set, selection, and package judgments whenever the reply, frame, bets, admitted field, or relevant weights change. Reevaluate and rerank immediately after confirmed voice disagreement.
Render and physically block weak fields; do not permit dominated live foils to create an apparently valid field.
Implement true automatic n=1 factory mode with provisional alternatives and an explicit student admission pick-list.
Replace Pick later with a functioning chooser and preserve the existing field until explicit picks are submitted.
Fix weight handling so zero remains zero, validate traced bases server-side, and surface near ties after every recomputation regardless of an older selection.
Harden irrelevant-use and prompt-injection admission independently of assignment keywords and apply untrusted-data boundaries to every student-controlled model input before any external call.

Add the R9c conviction-evidence intervention and make package prose accurately state when the student selected a non-leading bet; never label it the current leader unless the current ranking supports that claim.
Provide the specified phone-safe extraction repair, including tap-to-highlight or an explicit relevant-lines repaste flow that does not require editing the full thread.
Add keep-with-next pagination for PDF labels and regenerate the recommendation artifact so headings are not orphaned.
releaseRationale: Fail. The positive live workflows, confidence containment, scoped instructor access, and complete PDF leaves are established, but they do not close multiple authoritative source and artifact failures. The release permits client-state laundering and judgment bypass, omits required weak-field and one-bet behavior, mishandles corrections and confirmed voices, and ships a recommendation brief that contradicts its own current ranking. Each issue is actionable without changing the assignment, so the release contract forbids a pass.
